LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT DISCLOSES THAT SHE KNOWS MR. SULLWOLD AND WORKED WITH HIM IN THE 1980S OR 1990S WHILE EMPLOYED AT HOWARD RICE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT COMCAST OF CALIFORNIA III, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY AND NEGLIGENCE IN HER FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON EITHER CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S WITNESS MR. GRAZIANI HAD NO PERSONAL RESPONSIBILITY FOR SUPERVISING THE PROJECT OR ANY PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE OF THE WORK PERFORMED BY COMCAST OR OTHERS ON THE BLOCK WHERE THE INCIDENT OCCURRED. SEE GRAZIANI DEPOSITION AT PPS. 26:18-27:16; 91:10-22. DEFENDANTS' EVIDENCE IS INSUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT ONE OR MORE ELEMENTS OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY AND NEGLIGENCE CANNOT BE ESTABLISHED. (SEE CCP 437C(P)(2).) COMCAST'S WRITTEN OBJECTION TO EVIDENCE DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1354(B) (MUST "QUOTE OR SET FORTH THE OBJECTIONABLE STATEMENT OR MATERIAL"). ASSUMING COMCAST IS OBJECTING TO ALL OF PARAGRAPH 5, THE OBJECTION IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST TWO SENTENCES AND SUSTAINED AS TO THE REMAINING PARAGRAPH. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ARLENE HOWELL CSR2542.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VINCENT LEA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT PRODUCES SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO MEET HIS BURDEN THAT HE DID NOT HAVE ANY CONTROL OVER THE TRENCH IN THE SIDEWALK AT 2380 FOLSOM AND THAT HE ONLY INSTALLED TWO UTILITY JUNCTION BOXES ABOVE THE SIDEWALK ON OR ABOUT FEBRUARY 8, 2008 AND JUNE 25, 2008. (SEE DECLARATION OF LEA IN SUPPORT OF MOTION OF SUMMARY JUDGMENT, 1:27-2:8; EXHS. 1-5.). FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT STATES THAT HE NEVER REMOVED CONCRETE, OR EXCAVATED, DUG, OR WORKED ON THE PAVEMENT LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF 2380 FOLSOM, NOR DID HE PLACE A BOARD OR PLATFORM ON THE SIDEWALK. (ID. 3:1-8.) THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF, WHO HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO ANY MATERIAL FACT. DEFENDANT LEA'S PURPORTED INCONSISTENT STATEMENT REGARDING THE DATE HE INSTALLED THE SECOND BOX DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT LEA INSTALLED THE PLATFORM OR EVER BROKE THE PAVEMENT BY DIGGING OR EXCAVATING AT ANY TIME, LET ALONE ON OR ABOUT FEBRUARY 8 AND JUNE 25, 2008. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT THAT DEFENDANT DOES NOT OWN, CONTROL, MANAGE, OR MAINTAIN 2380 FOLSOM, OR THE SIDEWALKS ADJACENT TO OR NEAR IT. (SEE PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO LEA'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS 23.) THE DECLARATION OF ALEXANDER ALEXEEV IN OPPOSITION TO LEA'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THESE ISSUES OR IMPEACH DEFENDANT'S LEA CREDIBILITY. (ID. 8.) THE RECENTLY TAKEN DEPOSITION OF BRANDON LEA CORROBORATES DEFENDANT LEA'S TESTIMONY THAT HE DID NOT EXCAVATE OR WORK ON THE PAVEMENT. (SECOND SUPPL. DECL. OF MARCIA POLLIONI IN SUPPORT OF VINCENT LEA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, FILED AUGUST 20, 2013, EX. 2 AT PP. 7-8; 16-24.) THE COURT DECLINES PLAINTIFF'S FURTHER REQUEST TO DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(E). (PLEASE SEE "REMARK" BELOW FOR ADDITIONAL TEXT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARNOLD I. BERSCHLER, TEL. NO. 1-800-338-1441 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARCIA A. POLLIONI OF LONG BLUMBERG, LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 941-0090 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT VINCENT LEA;
ATTY. ANDREW GILL OF GILBERT & KELLY, TEL. NO. 352-6406 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT COMCAST OF CA;
ATTY. ROBERT T. SULLWOLD OF SULLWOLD & HUGHES, TEL. NO. 263-1850 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SF NETWORK MINISTRIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Vincent Lea's Motion for Summary Judgment as to the first and second causes of action in Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is granted. Defendant produces sufficient evidence to meet his burden that he did not have any control over the trench in the sidewalk at 2380 Folsom and that he only installed two utility junction boxes above the sidewalk on or about February 8, 2008 and June 25, 2008. (See Declaration of Lea in Support of Motion of Summary Judgment, 1:27-2:8; Exhs. 1-5.). Furthermore, Defendant states that he never removed concrete, or excavated, dug, or worked on the pavement located in the vicinity of 2380 Folsom, nor did he place a board or platform on the sidewalk. (Id. 3:1-8.) The burden shifted to Plaintiff, who has not shown that there is a triable issue as to any material fact. Defendant Lea's purported inconsistent statement regarding the date he installed the second box does not create a triable issue as to whether Defendant Lea installed the platform or ever broke the pavement by digging or excavating at any time, let alone on or about February 8 and June 25, 2008. Plaintiff does not dispute that that Defendant does not own, control, manage, or maintain 2380 Folsom, or the sidewalks adjacent to or near it. (See Plaintiff's Opposition to Lea's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 23.) The Declaration of Alexander Alexeev in Opposition to Lea's Summary Judgment Motion does not create a triable issue of fact on these issues or impeach Defendant's Lea credibility. (Id. 8.) The recently taken deposition of Brandon Lea corroborates Defendant Lea's testimony that he did not excavate or work on the pavement. (Second Suppl. Decl. of Marcia Pollioni in Support of Vincent Lea's Motion for Summary Judgment, filed August 20, 2013, Ex. 2 at pp. 7-8; 16-24.) The Court declines Plaintiff's further request to deny summary judgment pursuant to CCP 437c(e). The Court continued the hearing on August 2, 2013 to allow Plaintiff to take additional discovery, and Plaintiff has shown no basis to deny the motion at this point. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: A. BERSCHLER, ESQ., FOR PLAINTIFF MARIA RAMIREZ.
ROBERT SULLWOLD, ESQ., 415.263.1850, FOR DEFENDANT SF NETWORK MINISTRIES HOUSING CORPORATION.
MARCIA POLLIONI, ESQ., 925.941.0090, FOR DEFFENDANT VINCENT LEA.
PAUL BIGLEY, ESQ., 415.352.6409, FOR DEFENDANT COCAST OF CALIFORNIA III, INC.

THE COURT DISCLOSES THAT SHE KNOWS MR. SULLWOLD AND WORKED WITH HIM IN THE 1980S OR 1990S WHILE EMPLOYED AT HOWARD RICE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT COMCAST OF CALIFORNIA III, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiff's causes of action for premises liability and negligence in her First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is denied. Defendant has failed to shift the burden on either cause of action. Defendant's witness Mr. Graziani had no personal responsibility for supervising the project or any personal knowledge of the work performed by Comcast or others on the block where the incident occurred. See Graziani Deposition at pps. 26:18-27:16; 91:10-22. Defendants' evidence is insufficient to establish that one or more elements of the causes of action for premises liability and negligence cannot be established. (See CCP 437c(p)(2).) Comcast's written objection to evidence does not comply with CRC 3.1354(b) (must "quote or set forth the objectionable statement or material"). Assuming Comcast is objecting to all of paragraph 5, the objection is overruled as to the first two sentences and sustained as to the remaining paragraph. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR2542.
